description_long: This traffic barricade consists of a rectangular frame with two horizontal boards painted in alternating orange and white diagonal stripes, typical of roadwork or construction barriers. The boards are supported by two vertical legs, elevating the barricade above the ground. The surface appears to be made of painted wood or plastic, with the stripes serving as a high-visibility warning. There are no visible lights or electronic components, and the structure is lightweight enough to be manually repositioned. The barricade is designed to be placed across roads, sidewalks, or pathways to restrict or redirect traffic. Its construction is simple, with no additional signage or attachment fixtures visible.
description: A rectangular traffic barricade with orange and white diagonal stripes on two horizontal boards, supported by two vertical legs. It is lightweight, portable, and designed for high-visibility road or sidewalk use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the barricade with orange and white diagonal stripes and both support legs visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the barricade and the depth of the boards, with one leg visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the barricade, with the support structure and both legs visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other edge of the barricade and the thickness of the boards.
attribute_reflective_striping_pattern: Diagonal orange and white stripes, medium width, repeated across both horizontal boards.
